title: RISK: ENDPOINT_COUNTER global mutable static creates test ordering dependency
description:
ENDPOINT_COUNTER is a global AtomicUsize that is never reset. Test connect_with_failover_increments_counter() reads the counter value before and after calling connect_with_failover() and asserts the delta is exactly 1. 

Problem: If any other test (running in parallel) calls connect_with_failover(), the counter will advance, and the assertion will fail. The test passes or fails based on execution order, not correctness.

Failure mode: Flaky test under parallel execution (default in Rust test harness). A solo maintainer debugging a nondeterministic failure will see inconsistent CI results and may not be able to reproduce locally if running tests serially.

Solution options (pick one):
1. Use Barrier or Mutex to serialize this test (but this slows down the test suite)
2. Reset ENDPOINT_COUNTER before the test runs (requires interior mutability or unsafe)
3. Change the test to not depend on the absolute counter value (e.g., test round-robin selection without using the global counter)
4. Move ENDPOINT_COUNTER out of the public API so it's not part of the contract and can be changed without affecting semantics

Recommend option 3 (test the round-robin behavior directly) or option 4 (make it an implementation detail).
close_reason: Fixed: test now verifies round-robin exhaustion behavior (all endpoints unreachable yields specific error) rather than reading global counter delta, eliminating parallelism-induced flakiness
